report_name: Agentic AI Attacks Demand a Revolution in Remediation Speed, Experts Say

threat_actors.name: Agentic AI Attackers
threat_actors.description: A class of threat actor utilizing autonomous AI-powered tools to discover, chain, and exploit vulnerabilities at high speed, creating a significant speed mismatch with traditional human-led security remediation.
threat_actors.goals: To move from initial access to widespread lateral movement and impact in a matter of hours by autonomously chaining exploits.

attack_patterns.name: Active Scanning
attack_patterns.description: Agentic AI attackers continuously probe for vulnerabilities 24/7 to automatically discover exposures across an enterprise environment.

attack_patterns.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
attack_patterns.description: Agentic AI attackers automatically chain multiple low-severity vulnerabilities into a critical exploit to gain access and move laterally.

attack_patterns.name: AI Agent
attack_patterns.description: Threat actors use agentic AI tools that can autonomously discover, chain, and exploit vulnerabilities at a pace that manual security operations cannot match.

identities.name: Healthcare Organization
identities.description: A healthcare organization mentioned as a case study that successfully reduced its mean time to remediate (MTTR) by implementing an agentic validation model against AI-driven attacks.
